news.title: Mysuru civic body to adopt LiDAR tech to increase property tax collection
news.para: Mysuru City Corporation is adopting LiDAR technology to boost property tax collection. This advanced mapping system accurately identifies property boundaries, structures, and land use changes, aiming to increase revenue by 30% to Rs 300 crore. The technology, successfully used in other cities, will help bring unauthorized constructions under the tax net.

news.title: IFC, GBPN and Smarter Dharma collaborate on green materials platform to boost sustainable construction in India
news.para: India is experiencing a transformative shift in its urban development, with Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities emerging as hubs of opportunity. A new innovative platform, MGEM, is stepping up to support this growth by enhancing the adoption of green building practices. It delivers trusted green construction resources while facilitating access to financing aimed at sustainable development.

news.title: Alumil India outlines 2026 expansion plans
news.para: Alumil India will be unveiling new showrooms located in Ludhiana, New Delhi, and Calicut. This move is designed to foster closer ties with customers and address the growing need for high-quality aluminium systems essential for today's architectural endeavors.

news.title: Gurugram: Seven killed as mound of soil collapses at Signature Global Society's construction site
news.para: A tragic incident unfolded at a Gurugram construction site where a soil mound collapsed. Seven workers lost their lives, and four others sustained injuries. The victims were working on a Sewerage Treatment Plant when the mound gave way. Rescue operations were swift, with SDRF and NDRF teams reaching the scene. The injured were rushed to a hospital.

news.title: Seven establishments sealed over property tax dues by Lucknow civic body
news.para: The Lucknow Municipal Corporation has sealed seven government establishments in zones 2 and 4 for failing to pay substantial property and house tax dues. These establishments collectively owe over Rs 2.49 crore in pending taxes. Civic teams initiated sealing proceedings after issuing multiple notices to the defaulters.

news.title: Illegal sand mining busted in Yavatmal
news.para: Authorities busted a major illegal sand mining racket in Yavatmal, seizing 11 tipper trucks and heavy machinery worth over ₹1.42 crore. The operation at Rohini sand ghat revealed large-scale illegal excavation and transportation of sand beyond permissible hours. Meanwhile, Chandrapur police invoked MCOCA against a sand mafia gang involved in murder and other unlawful activities.

news.title: Joint panel set up in Haryana to probe illegal tree felling, construction in Raisina Aravalis
news.para: Authorities are investigating illegal tree felling and construction in Gurugram's Aravali region. A joint committee will inspect the Raisina village area. The National Green Tribunal directed action following environmental concerns. Previous efforts to restore the Aravalis have faced challenges. The committee will recommend measures to address violations and protect the ecological zone.

news.title: Cops submit second chargesheet against Radius Estates' promoter in Yes Bank cheating case
news.para: Builder Sanjay Chhabria, promoter of Radius Estates, faces a chargesheet for allegedly cheating Yes Bank of Rs 30.39 crore. Funds from a Rs 50-crore overdraft facility for a Mhada redevelopment project were diverted to related entities to repay unsecured loans and interest, violating loan conditions.

news.title: Exorbitantly priced homes beyond middle class: Punjab & Haryana HC
news.para: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has expressed serious concern over soaring property prices auctioned by government development agencies in Chandigarh, Panchkula, and Mohali, deeming them unaffordable for the middle class. The court has directed the states to propose measures, including draw of lots, to ensure affordable housing for all sections of society.

news.title: Greater Bengaluru authority issues final warning to 1,010 property tax defaulters
news.para: The Greater Bengaluru Authority has issued a final warning to 1,010 property owners with Rs 33.9 crore in outstanding property tax. The authority is intensifying property auctions for long-pending dues, with two properties already auctioned in KR Pura. Owners are urged to clear their taxes immediately to avoid legal action and property confiscation.

news.title: Highway expansion redraws Kochi’s real estate map
news.para: National highway construction is driving Kochi's real estate boom, shifting demand to affordable outskirts like Maradu and Koonammavu. Lower land prices are attracting investors and families seeking mid-range properties. This trend is also reflected in a dip in Kochi's House Price Index, with businesses favoring well-connected highway regions.

news.title: Mumbai: Flats for 252 Goregaon-Mulund Link Road project PAPs to be ready by March-end
news.para: Rehabilitation buildings for 252 project-affected persons in the Goregaon-Mulund Link Road project's first phase are nearing completion by March end. These compact 300-square-foot apartments at Kanjurmarg West will feature modern amenities, with construction of seven buildings progressing. Underground tunnel excavation is slated to begin in June 2026.

news.title: Lucknow: 549 plots allotted under land pooling policy in LDA’s IT City scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) held a lottery to allot residential plots to farmers and landowners who contributed land for the IT City scheme.  The draw, streamed live, allocated 549 plots of various sizes in sectors 15, 16, 17, 18, and 30.  Around 350 landowners pooled nearly 550 acres for the project.

news.title: CIDCO plans 120-acre commercial hub in Navi Mumbai, floats tender for 20-year development
news.para: CIDCO has invited bids to develop a 120-acre International Corporate Park in Navi Mumbai's Kharghar, aiming to create a new business district akin to BKC. This initiative, under the state's Theme-Based policy, seeks a private developer via competitive bidding to foster a world-class corporate ecosystem and boost Navi Mumbai's economic growth.

news.title: Truhome Finance files ₹3,000-crore IPO papers
news.para: Truhome Finance, backed by Warburg Pincus, is launching an IPO to raise Rs 3,000 crore. The housing finance company plans to use the funds to strengthen its capital base for future lending and meet regulatory requirements. This move signals significant growth plans for the affordable housing sector. The IPO includes a fresh issue and an offer for sale.

news.title: Pirojsha Adi Godrej buys nearly 1.8% stake in Arvind SmartSpaces for ₹40.7 crore
news.para: Pirojsha Adi Godrej has acquired a significant stake in Arvind SmartSpaces. He purchased nearly 1.8 percent of the real estate developer's shares. The transaction was valued at Rs 40.7 crore. This deal took place through an open market transaction. An affiliate of HDFC Capital sold the shares.

news.title: Calcutta HC cancels builder's bail, holds liberty of accused cannot be protected by order 'born in sin'
news.para: The Calcutta High Court has cancelled bail granted to a builder in a cheating case. The court found the bail order to be procedurally flawed and lacking in reasoning. Justice Uday Kumar stated that liberty cannot be protected by an order that is 'born in sin'.

news.title: Jewar airport to be inaugurated soon: Civil aviation minister
news.para: Jewar airport in Uttar Pradesh has received its aerodrome licence and will begin operations shortly. The Civil Aviation Minister announced this development in the Rajya Sabha. The government is committed to expanding India's airport network, with plans for new airports and upgrades across the country. This initiative aims to boost regional connectivity and accommodate growing air travel demand.

news.title: Jaipur: About 2,000 Sanganer residents march to protect homes
news.para: Nearly 2,000 residents from 87 Sanganer colonies protested a housing board land acquisition, fearing for their homes. Families have lived in these colonies for years, built houses with bank loans, and developed civic facilities. Residents issued a 15-day ultimatum to the state government for a resolution, threatening intensified agitation if their concerns are not addressed.

news.title: Raymond Realty to jointly develop Mumbai project; County Group unveils ₹1,200 crore project in Noida
news.para: Raymond Realty is developing a new residential project in Kandivali, Mumbai. This project has a gross development value of ₹3,000 crore. Meanwhile, County Group has launched Clove County, a luxury residential project in Noida. This project features 226 apartments with prices ranging from ₹10 crore to ₹18 crore.
